expectations.text: A new checkpoint commit was created on top of the initial skeleton commit.
The commit subject is a single substantive sentence (not 'Updates', 'wip', or empty).
expectations.evidence: Subject is a full sentence ending in a period.

expectations.text: CHECKPOINTS.md exists at the repo root and contains exactly one session entry matching the commit subject character-for-character.
The CHECKPOINTS.md entry is prefixed with today's date in YYYY-MM-DD format.
The commit body mentions the rejected functools.lru_cache alternative and explains the reasoning.
expectations.evidence: 'Rejected: lru_cache on _load_from_db()' section covers it with out-of-band mutation reasoning.

expectations.text: The commit body mentions the sorted()/generator dead end.
expectations.evidence: 'The sort bug (dead end worth remembering)' section.

expectations.text: The commit body references specific file paths that were touched.
config.draft.yml was NOT added to the commit.
The commit body is substantive: more than 400 characters.
A new git commit was created even though no source files changed.
expectations.evidence: Commit created with CHECKPOINTS.md as only diff.

expectations.text: The commit subject names the root cause (nginx/cookie/domain/staging).
CHECKPOINTS.md exists and contains one dated entry matching the commit subject.
The commit body explains the root cause (cookie domain / nginx proxy).
The commit body references infra/nginx/staging.conf as the fix location.
The commit body states that app/auth.py is correct and was NOT changed.
expectations.evidence: 'app/auth.py confirmed correct — do NOT modify it'.

expectations.text: The commit body mentions the Monday maintenance window plan.
The commit body is substantive: more than 400 characters.
Response shows evidence of reading CHECKPOINTS.md.
Response identifies the nginx/cookie/staging session as 'the auth thing'.
expectations.evidence: Response references Monday's nginx plan from the staging logout checkpoint.

expectations.text: Response retrieves the full commit body via git log --grep or git show.
expectations.evidence: commands-run.txt has git log --all --grep with the exact title.

expectations.text: Response mentions app/auth.py is correct and doesn't need changes.
expectations.evidence: 'app/auth.py is fine ... Do NOT touch it'.

expectations.text: Response points to infra/nginx/staging.conf as the fix location.
Response mentions proxy_cookie_domain or the Monday plan.
Response summarises past session back to user before making changes.
Response does NOT pull the wrong session (marketing or CSV).
expectations.evidence: No mention of styles.css or import_csv.py.
